reasoning_model_1: The user's command was to read the specific sentence '每个字，都要读清楚。' with extremely slow speed. Model 1 failed to strictly follow the content instructions, instead generating a conversational response: '好的，我会用极慢速，每个字都读清楚，你等着哦' (Okay, I will use extremely slow speed, read every word clearly, you wait). This constitutes a major content error as it synthesizes a chat reply rather than the target script. Furthermore, within the embedded attempt, it omitted the character '要' from the target text (saying '每个字都读清楚' instead of '每个字，都要读清楚'). regarding the feature '语速' (Speech Rate), the speech is indeed slow (approx 00:00-00:13 for the whole phrase), with pauses like at 00:06-00:07, but the failure in content accuracy is the primary issue. According to the scoring criteria, voice content clearly inconsistent with the script results in a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. It read exactly the target text '每个字，都要读清楚。' without any hallucinations, omissions, or conversational fillers. regarding the feature '语速' (Speech Rate), the model executed the 'extremely slow' command by enunciating each character individually with distinct spacing, as seen in the timestamps: '每' (00:00), '个' (00:01), '字' (00:02), '都' (00:03), '要' (00:04), '读' (00:05), '清' (00:06), '楚' (00:07). This results in a pace of roughly one character per second, which effectively captures the requirement for 'extremely slow' speed and 'reading every word clearly'. The delivery is stable and compliant.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental task of Text-to-Speech synthesis by treating the prompt as a chat conversation and generating a meta-response ('Okay, I will...') rather than reading the requested text. It also missed a character in the target phrase. Model 2, in contrast, adhered strictly to the requested text and perfectly implemented the 'extremely slow' and 'clear enunciation' constraints by adopting a slow, syllable-by-syllable pacing. Model 2 demonstrates superior instruction following and feature control.
